issue_title: ngcc timeout does not quit process
issue_body:
# 🐞 Bug report ### Command (mark with an `x`) - [ ] new - [x] build - [ ] serve - [ ] test - [ ] e2e - [ ] generate - [ ] add - [ ] update - [ ] lint - [ ] xi18n - [ ] run - [ ] config - [ ] help - [ ] version - [ ] doc ### Is this a regression? I don't know. ### Description ngcc timeout does not stop the execution and the process will run infinitely. I have seen this behavior on our CI, where the build runs since 3 days. The error occurs if there are multiple ng builds in parallel. ## 🔬 Minimal Reproduction 1. run a long running ngcc process 2. run another ngcc process which would run into the 25 seconds timeout. ## 🔥 Exception or Error 

[2020-04-11T11:56:45.990Z] > ng build "portal" "--prod" "--progress=false" "--i18n-locale=de" "--source-map"
[2020-04-11T11:57:09.822Z] Another process, with id 23892, is currently running ngcc.
[2020-04-11T11:57:09.822Z] Waiting up to 25s for it to finish.
[2020-04-11T11:57:36.823Z] Error: Timed out waiting 25s for another ngcc process, with id 23892, to complete.
[2020-04-11T11:57:36.823Z] (If you are sure no ngcc process is running then you should delete the lock-file at /.../node_modules/@angular/compiler-cli/ngcc/__ngcc_lock_file__.)
[2020-04-11T11:57:36.823Z]     at AsyncLocker. (/.../node_modules/@angular/compiler-cli/ngcc/src/locking/async_locker.js:91:31)
[2020-04-11T11:57:36.823Z]     at step (/.../node_modules/tslib/tslib.js:139:27)
[2020-04-11T11:57:36.823Z]     at Object.next (/.../node_modules/tslib/tslib.js:120:57)
[2020-04-11T11:57:36.823Z]     at fulfilled (/.../node_modules/tslib/tslib.js:110:62)
// end
## 🌍 Your Environment 

Angular CLI: 9.1.1
Node: 12.16.1
OS: win32 x64

Angular: 9.1.1
... animations, cli, common, compiler, compiler-cli, core, forms
... language-service, localize, platform-browser
... platform-browser-dynamic, router
Ivy Workspace: Yes

Package                            Version
------------------------------------------------------------
@angular-devkit/architect          0.901.1
@angular-devkit/build-angular      0.901.1
@angular-devkit/build-ng-packagr   0.901.1
@angular-devkit/build-optimizer    0.901.1
@angular-devkit/build-webpack      0.901.1
@angular-devkit/core               9.1.1
@angular-devkit/schematics         9.1.1
@ngtools/webpack                   9.1.1
@schematics/angular                9.1.1
@schematics/update                 0.901.1
ng-packagr                         9.1.0
rxjs                               6.5.4
typescript                         3.8.3
webpack                            4.42.0
**Anything else relevant?** I think the spawned ngcc does not return correctly, because the error "NGCC failed" is missing. https://github.com/angular/angular-cli/blob/00bf30650507b9f5d6c1cf91b483b8b64b98e096/packages/ngtools/webpack/src/ngcc_processor.ts#L75-L97 _EDIT:_ If the process is killed by Jenkins the output looks like: 

[2020-04-14T07:18:27.026Z] Sending interrupt signal to process
[2020-04-14T07:18:27.185Z] Sending interrupt signal to process
[2020-04-14T07:18:27.226Z] Sending interrupt signal to process
[2020-04-14T07:18:27.262Z] Sending interrupt signal to process
[2020-04-14T07:18:28.154Z] An unhandled exception occurred: NGCC failed.
But it also does not return a non zero return code, the script execution is successful.
pr_title: fix(ngcc): force ngcc to exit on error
pr_body:
For some reason (possibly related to async/await promises)
the ngcc process is not exiting when spawned from the CLI
when there has been an error (such as when it timesout waiting
for a lockfile to become free).

Calling `process.exit()` directly fixes this.

Fixes #36616
